blocks:
سُورة الطلاق

مدنيّة وهي 12 آية

سورة الطلاق

وتسمّى سورة النساء القصرى مدنية بالإجماع عدد آيها اثنتا عشرة آية بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ

(١) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُ ** (1) O Prophet! In al-Qummi, the address is to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the meaning is for the people. إِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَ ** (When you divorce women, divorce them at the commencement of their term) (Meaning) the time of their waiting period (عدّتهن), which is the state of purity (الطهر). In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: The waiting period (العدّة) is the state of purity (الطهر) from menstruation (المحيض). In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and al-Sajjad and al-Sadiq عليهما السلام: Divorce them before their waiting period (عدّتهنّ). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said that the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said: If a man intends divorce (الطلاق), he divorces her before her waiting period (عدّتها) without having intercourse (جماع). And from al-Baqir عليه السلام: Divorce (الطلاق) is only that he says to her, before the waiting period (العدّة), after she becomes pure from her menstruation (حيضها), before he has intercourse with her (يجامعها): "You are divorced (طالق)" or "Observe the waiting period (اعتدّي)," intending thereby the divorce (الطلاق), and he calls two just men (رجلين عدلين) to witness that. وَأَحْصُوا الْعِدَّةَ ** (and calculate the term) Keep strict account of it (اضبطوها) and complete it (which is) three menstrual cycles (قروء). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ رَبَّكُمْ ** (and be wary of Allah, your Lord.) (Be wary) regarding the prolongation of the waiting period (العدّة) and causing them harm. لا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ مِنْ بُيُوتِهِنَ ** (Do not turn them out of their houses) From their dwellings (مساكنهنّ) at the time of separation until their waiting period (عدّتهنّ) expires. وَلا يَخْرُجْنَ. (nor shall they leave) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: What is meant by this is the woman who is divorced once after another divorce (تطليقة بعد تطليقة). That is the one who does not leave until she is divorced the third time. If she is divorced the third time, she is irrevocably separated (بانت) from him, and she has no maintenance (نفقة) for her. As for the woman whom the man divorces once (تطليقة) and then leaves her until her term expires (يخلو أجلها), she also remains in her husband's house, and she has maintenance (النفقة) and lodging (السكنى) until her waiting period (عدّتها) expires. ** إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ. (unless they commit a gross indecency.) In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked about it, and he said: "Unless she commits adultery (تزني), then she is expelled and the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) is carried out upon her." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Rida عليه السلام, he said: (It is) her harming the man's family and her bad character (سوء خلقها). And from him عليه السلام: He means by the gross indecency (الفاحشة المبيّنة) that she harms her husband's family. If she does that, then if he wishes to expel her before her waiting period (عدّتها) expires, he may do so. In al-Majma', narrated from him and from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq عليهم السلام: (There is) that which is similar in meaning. And in al-Qummi, the meaning of the indecency (الفاحشة) is that she commits adultery (تزني) or exposes herself to men (تشرف على الرجال). And among the indecency (الفاحشة) is insolence (السّلاطة) towards her husband. If she commits any of that, it is lawful (حلّ) for him to expel her. In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Master of the Age (صاحب الزمان) عليه السلام: The gross indecency (الفاحشة المبيّنة) is tribadism (السّحق) short of adultery (الزنى)—the hadith continues. وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللهِ وَمَنْ يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ اللهِ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُ ** (These are the bounds of Allah, and whoever transgresses the bounds of Allah has certainly wronged himself,) by exposing it (his soul) to punishment (العقاب). لا تَدْرِي ** (You do not know,) (Meaning) the soul (النفس). لَعَلَّ اللهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَمْراً ** (maybe Allah will bring about a new situation after that.) And that is the desire for the divorced woman, either by taking her back (رجعة) or by a new marriage (استيناف). Al-Qummi said: Perhaps it will occur to her husband regarding the divorce (الطلاق) that he should take her back (فيراجعها). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: It is preferred for the jurist (الفقيه) when he wishes to divorce his wife that he divorces her with the divorce of the Sunnah (طلاق السنّة). Then he said: And that is what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: لَعَلَّ اللهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَمْراً (maybe Allah will bring about a new situation after that), meaning, after the divorce (الطلاق) and the expiration of the waiting period (العدّة), marrying her before she marries another husband. And from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The divorced woman (المطلّقة) may apply kohl (تكتحل), dye her hands with henna (تختضب), use perfume (تطيب), and wear whatever clothes she wishes, because Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: لَعَلَّ اللهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ أَمْراً (maybe Allah will bring about a new situation after that), perhaps she will enter his heart (تقع في نفسه) and he will take her back (فيراجعها).

(٢) فَإِذا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَ ** they approach the end of their waiting period (عدّتهنّ). فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَ ** then retain them take them back (راجعوهنّ). بِمَعْرُوفٍ ** with fairness with good companionship (حسن عشرة) and suitable expenditure (انفاق مناسب). أَوْ فارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ ** or separate from them with fairness by fulfilling the right (إيفاء الحقّ), providing enjoyment (التمتيع), and avoiding harm (اتّقاء الضّرار). وَأَشْهِدُوا ذَوَيْ عَدْلٍ مِنْكُمْ ** and take as witness two just men from among yourselves concerning the divorce (الطلاق).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said that this verse] is grammatically connected (معطوف) to His saying: إِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَ. (When you divorce women, divorce them at the commencement of their waiting period.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), He said to Abu Yusuf al-Qadi: "Indeed, Allah, blessed and exalted is He, commanded divorce in His Book and emphasized it with two witnesses, and He was not pleased with them unless they were two just men (عدلين). And He commanded marriage (التزويج) in His Book, but He left it without witnesses (بلا شهود). Yet, you established two witnesses in that which He left without mention (أهمل) and invalidated the two witnesses in that which He emphasized (أكّد)." وَأَقِيمُوا الشَّهادَةَ And establish the testimony O witnesses (أيّها الشهود) when needed. لِلَّهِ ** for Allah purely for His Countenance (خالصاً لوجهه). ذلِكُمْ يُوعَظُ بِهِ مَنْ كانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجاً.** This is what anyone who believes in Allah and the Last Day is advised to follow. And whoever is wary of Allah, He shall appoint a way out for him.

(٣) وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَحْتَسِبُ. (And provide for him from whence he does not reckon.) Al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام): He said: In his worldly life (في دنياه). In al-Majma' narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله): That He recited it and said: مَخْرَجاً (a way out) from the ambiguities (شبهات) of the world, and from the agonies (غمرات) of death, and the hardships (شدائد) of the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). And from him (عليه السلام): "I certainly know an Ayah (آية) that if people were to adopt it, it would suffice them: وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ (And whoever is wary of Allah) the Ayah." And He kept saying it and repeating it. And in Nahj al-Balagha: مَخْرَجاً (a way out) from trials (الفتن) and a light (نوراً) from darkness (الظلم). In al-Majma' narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام): Regarding وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لا يَحْتَسِبُ (And provide for him from whence he does not reckon), meaning: He blesses him (يبارك له) in what has come to him (فيما أتاه). In al-Faqih narrated from him, from his forefathers, from Ali (عليهم السلام): Whoever is brought provision (رزق) by Allah, and he did not step toward it with his foot (برجله), nor extend his hand toward it (يده), nor speak about it with his tongue (بلسانه), nor prepare his garments (ثيابه) for it, nor expose himself to it, he is among those whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), mentioned in His Book: وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ (And whoever is wary of Allah) the Ayah. In al-Kafi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (عليه السلام): That when this Ayah was revealed, a group (قوماً) of the companions (أصحاب) of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) closed their doors and devoted themselves to worship (العبادة), saying: "We have been sufficed." This reached the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله), so He sent for them and said: "What compelled you to do what you have done?" They replied: "O Messenger of Allah, He has guaranteed our provisions (أرزاقنا), so we devoted ourselves to worship." He said: "Indeed, whoever does that, it will not be answered for him. You must seek [provision] (عليكم بالطّلب)." And from him (عليه السلام): These are a group (قوم) of our weak followers (شيعتنا ضعفاء) who do not possess what they can use to travel to us (يتحمّلون به إلينا). So they listen to our narrations (حديثنا) and acquire knowledge (علمنا) from us. Then a group above them travels, spending their wealth (أموالهم) and tiring their bodies (أبدانهم) until they come to us and hear our narrations, and then they transmit them (فينقلوه) to [the weak followers]. So, these [weak followers] preserve it (يعيه هؤلاء) while those [who traveled] waste it (يضيّعه هؤلاء). Those are the ones for whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), makes a way out (مخرجاً) and provides for them from whence they do not reckon. ** وَمَنْ يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ ** (And whoever relies on Allah, He will suffice him.) He is sufficient for him (كافيه). ** إِنَّ اللهَ بالِغُ أَمْرِهِ ** (Indeed, Allah is the executor of His command.) He achieves what He wills (ما يريده), and no desired thing escapes Him (ولا يفوته مراد). And it was read with the idafa (بالإضافة) (genitive construction). ** قَدْ جَعَلَ اللهُ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدْراً ** (Allah has set a measure for everything.) A determination (تقديراً) or a measure (مقداراً) that does not change (لا يتغيّر). And this is a clarification of the necessity of reliance (وجوب التوكّل), a confirmation of the preceding rulings (الأحكام), and a preparation for the measures (المقادير) that will follow. In al-Kafi narrated from Imam al-Kazim (عليه السلام): That He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "Reliance upon Allah (التوكّل على الله) has degrees (درجات). Among them is that you rely upon Allah in all your affairs (أمورك كلّها). Whatever He does to you, you are content with it (راضياً), knowing that He will not withhold good (خيراً) or favor (فضلاً) from you, and knowing that this is for Him. So, rely upon Allah by entrusting that to Him (بتفويض ذلك إليه), and trust Him in it and in other matters." And in al-Ma'ani narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): Gabriel (جبرائيل) came to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله). The Prophet said to him: "O Gabriel, what is reliance upon Allah (التوكّل على الله)?" He replied: "It is the knowledge that the created being (المخلوق) neither harms nor benefits, neither gives nor withholds, and the practice of despairing of creation (اليأس من الخلق). When the servant is like that, he does not rely upon anyone other than Allah, nor does he hope for or fear anyone other than Allah, nor does he covet anyone other than Allah. This, then, is reliance (التوكّل)."

(٤) وَاللَّائِي يَئِسْنَ مِنَ الْمَحِيضِ مِنْ نِسائِكُمْ ** (And as for those of your women who have ceased menstruating—if you are in doubt—) so they do not menstruate إِنِ ارْتَبْتُمْ ** you doubt concerning their matter, meaning you are ignorant, so you do not know whether their menstruation ceased due to old age or due to an ailment (عارض).

In al-Majma', narrated from our Imams (peace be upon them): They are those whose likes menstruate, because if they were at the age of those who do not menstruate, there would be no meaning for the doubt (الارتياب). ** فَعِدَّتُهُنَّ ثَلاثَةُ أَشْهُرٍ (their term shall be three months,) It is narrated: When وَالْمُطَلَّقاتُ يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنْفُسِهِنَّ ثَلاثَةُ قُرُوءٍ ** (And the divorced women shall wait by themselves for three courses) was revealed, it was asked: "What is the waiting period (عدّة) for those who do not menstruate?" So وَاللَّائِي لَمْ يَحِضْنَ (and also for those who have not menstruated) was revealed, meaning: And those who have not yet menstruated are likewise. وَأُولاتُ الْأَحْمالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَنْ يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَ. (And the term of the pregnant ones is when they deliver their burden.)

In al-Majma', narrated from them (peace be upon them): It applies specifically to divorce (الطلاق).

I say: Meaning, excluding death, for their waiting period (عدّة) in that case is the longer of the two terms (أبعد الأجلين).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who divorced his wife while she was pregnant, and she had two (fetuses) in her womb. She delivered one, and one remained. He said: She becomes irrevocably divorced (تبين) with the first (delivery), but she is not lawful for husbands until she delivers what remains in her womb.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a pregnant woman whose husband dies, and she delivers and marries before four months and ten days have passed for her. He said: If he (the second husband) had consummated the marriage (دخل بها), they shall be separated, and then she shall never be lawful for him. She must complete the remaining waiting period (عدّة) from the first (husband's death), and then begin a new waiting period from the latter (second husband) of three courses (ثلاثة قروء). And if he had not consummated the marriage, they shall be separated, and she must complete the remaining waiting period from the first (husband's death), and he (the second husband) is one of the suitors (خاطب من الخطّاب). ** وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ ** regarding His rulings, observing their rights يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ يُسْراً ** He will make his affair easy for him and grant him success (يوفّقه) in goodness.

(٥) ذلِكَ ** is a reference (إشارة) to what was mentioned of the rulings (الأحكام). أَمْرُ اللهِ أَنْزَلَهُ إِلَيْكُمْ وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللهَ ** (5) That is the command of Allah which He has sent down to you. And whoever is wary of Allah regarding His command (أمره), يُكَفِّرْ عَنْهُ سَيِّئاتِهِ ** He will absolve him of his misdeeds, for indeed, good deeds (الحسنات) remove (يذهبن) bad deeds (السيئات). وَيُعْظِمْ لَهُ أَجْراً (and magnify his reward) by multiplication (بالمضاعفة).

(٦) ** أَسْكِنُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ سَكَنْتُمْ (6) Lodge them where you are lodged, meaning, a place from your dwelling (سُكْنَاكُمْ). مِنْ وُجْدِكُمْ according to your means, according to your capacity (وُسْعِكُمْ). وَلا تُضآرُّوهُنَ and do not harass them regarding the dwelling (السُّكْنَى), لِتُضَيِّقُوا عَلَيْهِنَ** to put them in straits. so that you compel them to leave.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A man should not harass his wife when he divorces her, putting her in straits until she moves out before her waiting period (عِدَّة) expires, for Allah has forbidden that. Then He (peace be upon him) recited this Ayah: وَإِنْ كُنَّ أُولاتِ حَمْلٍ فَأَنْفِقُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ حَتَّى يَضَعْنَ حَمْلَهُنَ (If they are pregnant, maintain them until they deliver their burden.) so that they leave the waiting period (الْعِدَّة). Al-Qummi said: The divorced woman whom the husband has the right to take back (رَجْعَة) has the right to dwelling (سُكْنَى) and maintenance (نَفَقَة) from him as long as she is in the waiting period (الْعِدَّة). If she is pregnant, he must maintain her until she delivers her burden.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The woman divorced three times (ثَلَاثًا) does not have maintenance (نَفَقَة) due from her husband; rather, it is only the woman whom her husband has the right to take back (رَجْعَة).

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the woman divorced three times (ثَلَاثًا): Does she have maintenance (النَّفَقَة) and dwelling (السُّكْنَى)? He said: "Is she pregnant (أَحُبْلَى)?" It was said: "No." He said: "Then no." And there are other narratives (أَخْبَار) with the same meaning. فَإِنْ أَرْضَعْنَ لَكُمْ If they suckle [the baby] for you, after the bond of marriage (عَلَقَة النِّكَاح) has been severed, فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَ give them their wages, for the suckling (الْإِرْضَاع). وَأْتَمِرُوا بَيْنَكُمْ بِمَعْرُوفٍ and consult together honourably. and let some of you consult others with kindness (بِجَمِيل) regarding the suckling (الْإِرْضَاع) and the wage (الْأَجْر). وَإِنْ تَعاسَرْتُمْ But if you make things difficult for one another, you become constrained (تَضَايَقْتُمْ), فَسَتُرْضِعُ لَهُ أُخْرى then another woman shall suckle [the baby] for him. another woman (امْرَأَة أُخْرَى). And in this is a reproach (مُعَاتَبَة) to the mother for making things difficult (الْمُعَاسَرَة).

(٧) لِيُنْفِقْ ذُو سَعَةٍ مِنْ سَعَتِهِ وَمَنْ قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُ فَلْيُنْفِقْ مِمَّا آتاهُ اللهُ ** (7) Let the man of means spend out of his means, and let him whose provision is restricted spend out of what Allah has given him. Meaning, let every person, whether affluent (الموسر) or poor (المعسر), spend what his capacity allows him. لا يُكَلِّفُ اللهُ نَفْساً إِلَّا ما آتاها ** Allah does not task any soul except according to what He has given it. Except its capacity (وسعها). And in this is a soothing of the heart of the poor person (المعسر). سَيَجْعَلُ اللهُ بَعْدَ عُسْرٍ يُسْراً ** Allah will bring about ease after hardship. Meaning, soon and later (عاجلاً وآجلاً). And this ruling applies to every expenditure (انفاق).

So, in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the affluent man (الرجل الموسر) who acquires many fine garments, tayalisah (الطّيالسة), and many shirts, protecting some with others, using them for adornment. Is he extravagant (مسرف)? He said: "No, because Allah says: لِيُنْفِقْ ذُو سَعَةٍ مِنْ سَعَتِهِ. (Let the man of means spend out of his means.)"

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: وَمَنْ قُدِرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقُهُ فَلْيُنْفِقْ مِمَّا آتاهُ ** (and let him whose provision is restricted spend out of what Allah has given him). He said: "If the man spends upon his wife what sustains her (ما يقيم ظهرها) along with clothing (كسوة), [it is sufficient]; otherwise, separation (فرّق) shall occur between them."

(٨) وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ (8) How many a town (meaning) the people of a town. ** عَتَتْ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّها وَرُسُلِهِ ** rebelled against the command of its Lord and His apostles! (meaning) It turned away from it with the turning away (إعراض) of the rebellious (العاتي). ** فَحاسَبْناها حِساباً شَدِيداً ** So We settled account with it with a severe reckoning, (meaning) by thorough investigation (بالاستقصاء) and close scrutiny (والمناقشة). ** وَعَذَّبْناها عَذاباً نُكْراً ** and We punished it with a dire punishment. (meaning) dreadful (منكراً), or what is meant is either the reckoning and punishment of the Hereafter (and He expressed it in the past tense because of its certainty (لتحقّقه)), or the thorough investigation (استقصاء) of their sins and what afflicted them immediately (عاجلاً).

(٩) فَذاقَتْ وَبالَ أَمْرِها ** (9) so they tasted the evil consequence of their conduct, the punishment for their disbelief and their acts of disobedience وَكانَ عاقِبَةُ أَمْرِها خُسْراً ** and the consequence of their conduct was ruin. there was absolutely no profit in it. (١٠) أَعَدَّ اللهُ لَهُمْ عَذاباً شَدِيداً فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ يا أُولِي الْأَلْبابِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَدْ أَنْزَلَ اللهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ذِكْراً. (10) Allah has prepared for them a severe punishment. So be wary of Allah, O men of understanding who have faith! Allah has certainly sent down to you a Reminder, (١١) رَسُولاً يَتْلُوا عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِ اللهِ مُبَيِّناتٍ. (11) a Messenger reciting to you the manifest signs of Allah,

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: فَسْئَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ ** (So ask the People of the Reminder): "Verily, the Reminder (الذِّكْر) is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and we are His people (أَهْلُهُ*)." He said: "And that is manifest in the Book of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, where He says in Surat al-Talaq: فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ يا أُولِي الْأَلْبابِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قَدْ أَنْزَلَ اللهُ إِلَيْكُمْ ذِكْراً رَسُولاً يَتْلُوا عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِ اللهِ مُبَيِّناتٍ لِيُخْرِجَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ (So be wary of Allah, O men of understanding who have faith! Allah has certainly sent down to you a Reminder, a Messenger reciting to you the manifest signs of Allah, that He may bring those who have faith and do righteous deeds out of darkness into light.)" (meaning) from misguidance to guidance. وَمَنْ يُؤْمِنْ بِاللهِ وَيَعْمَلْ صالِحاً يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً ** (And whoever has faith in Allah and acts righteously, He will admit him into gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them forever.) And it was read نُدْخِلْهُ (We will admit him) with the ن (nūn). قَدْ أَحْسَنَ اللهُ لَهُ رِزْقاً. (Allah has certainly made a good provision for him.)

(١٢) اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ وَمِنَ الْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَ ** (12) Allah is He Who created seven heavens, and of the earth their like. وَمِنَ الْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَ in number. يَتَنَزَّلُ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَ ** The command descends between them. The command (أمر) of Allah and His decree (قضاؤه) flows between them, and His judgment (حكمه) is executed within them. لِتَعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ وَأَنَّ اللهَ قَدْ أَحاطَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عِلْماً ** so that you may know that Allah has power over all things, and that Allah encompasses all things in knowledge. This is the reason (علّة) for the creation, or for the descent (يتنزّل), or for what encompasses both of them, for each of the two matters indicates the perfection of His power (قدرته) and His knowledge (علمه).

Al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: وَالسَّماءِ ذاتِ الْحُبُكِ ** By the heaven full of pathways, He said: "It is woven (محبوكة) to the earth," and he interlaced his fingers. Then he explained the manner (كيفيّة) of the creation of the seven heavens (السماوات السّبع) and the seven earths (الأرضين السّبع) and their interweaving (اشتباكهما), and that the lowest heaven (السماء الدنيا) is a dome (قبّة) over this earth, and that the second earth is above the lowest heaven, and the second heaven is a dome over it, and so on until the seventh of both of them. Then he said: And this is the saying of Allah: الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ وَمِنَ الْأَرْضِ مِثْلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنَهُنَ ** Who created seven heavens, and of the earth their like. The command descends between them. He said: "As for the Master of the Command (صاحب الأمر), He is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and the Successor (الوصيّ) after him, standing upon the face of the earth. The command (الأمر) only descends to him from above the heaven, between the heavens and the earths." The complete narration (تمام الحديث) has already passed in its entirety in Surat al-Dhariyat.

In Thawab al-A'mal and Al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surat al-Talaq and al-Tahrim in his obligatory prayer (فريضة), Allah will grant him refuge from being among those who fear or grieve on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), and he will be cured (عوفي) from the Fire, and Allah will admit him into Paradise (الجنّة) due to his recitation of them and his preservation of them, because they belong to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله."
